file_verdicts.summary: makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.

audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/mendeleydesktop-bundled.git to /tmp/aur-sleuth/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-53dgbukz...
Cloned repository to /tmp/aur-sleuth/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-53dgbukz
Analyzing mendeleydesktop-bundled AUR package (working in /tmp/aur-sleuth/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-53dgbukz) with qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 from https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines standard source URLs hosted on the official Mendeley domain (`desktop-download.mendeley.com`), which are appropriate for the package. The `sha512sums` are explicitly listed and match the respective architecture-specific tarballs, ensuring integrity. There is no command substitution, execution of downloaded code, or network operations in the global scope or in `pkgver()`, which is absent (so default `pkgver` is used). The conditional block modifying `CARCH` is limited to variable assignment and does not invoke external commands or perform side effects beyond packaging logic. Since `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` only sources the PKGBUILD and fetches sources, and no malicious code is executed during sourcing, this step is safe. Content within the `package()` function is irrelevant for this gate, as it will not be executed.
</details>
<summary>
No malicious execution during source fetch and parse</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious execution during source fetch and parse[/]
Running makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare to download sources for agent...
makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.
! makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.
Audit complete! Result: Skipped -- REVIEW MANUALLY
(Skipped 1 file: makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.)

API Usage Summary
  Models: qwen/qwen3-235b-a22b-2507
  Prompt Tokens: 2,873
  Completion Tokens: 219
  Total Tokens: 3,092
  Total Cost: $0.000280
  Execution Time: 7.67 seconds
Full audit report can be found in /tmp/aur-sleuth/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-report-mendeleydesktop-bundled.txt
Final Status: SKIPPED


No issues found.


Audit Skips:

[SKIPPED] makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.

data.reasoning: Both models successfully performed the initial PKGBUILD audit gate and found no malicious code in the global scope or pkgver() — sources come from the official Mendeley domain (desktop-download.mendeley.com) over HTTPS with pinned SHA-512 checksums, and no command substitution or network execution occurs during sourcing. However, neither model was able to proceed past source download (makepkg --nobuild failed), so 0 files were actually reviewed beyond the PKGBUILD. The prepare(), build(), and package() functions and any .install hooks were never examined. The PKGBUILD itself appears benign at the parse level, but a full safety verdict cannot be reached without reviewing the install logic and any packaged scripts. Verdict is inconclusive due to incomplete coverage.
data.disagreement_analysis: No substantive disagreement. Both models reached the same conclusion: PKGBUILD safe to source, but audit skipped because source download failed. The trigger note ('shallow, reviewed 0 files') reflects both models hitting the same makepkg download failure, likely because the upstream Mendeley download URLs for v1.19.2 are no longer reachable (dead links for an old version).
coverage_issues: Zero files reviewed beyond the initial PKGBUILD parse-gate; prepare(), build(), package() functions not analyzed
Any .install hooks (e.g., post_install/post_upgrade) were never reviewed — common location for risky behavior in bundled desktop apps
Source download failure prevented inspection of any bundled scripts/binaries shipped in the source tarballs
false_positives: The 'syntactically odd' $CARCH=i486 observation by deepseek is correctly identified as non-malicious; not a finding, just noted as benign — appropriate handling
false_negatives: Neither model could assess the package() function which, for a 'bundled' proprietary app, typically copies/installs prebuilt binaries and may include install hooks — these were not reviewed but this is a forced gap due to download failure, not a missed file in an available tree
re_audit_focus: PKGBUILD package() and prepare() functions once sources are obtainable
Source URL availability — confirm whether the official Mendeley v1.19.2 download links are dead (outdated package) vs a transient failure
learnings: Source download failures should trigger a fallback: even without downloaded tarballs, the audit can statically review the full PKGBUILD (all functions) and any committed .install files in the cloned AUR repo rather than reporting 0 files reviewed

The audit tool should distinguish 'sources unreachable' (likely stale upstream URL for an old version) from genuine security skips — a stale-link failure is a maintenance/availability concern, not a safety verdict

When makepkg --nobuild fails, the tool should still parse and present the .install hook and package()/prepare() function bodies from the local checkout for manual or LLM review, since those are local files independent of remote sources
Add a heuristic to flag 'bundled' packages for extra scrutiny of install hooks and binary-copy logic, as these commonly bypass source review
